channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Users beware: This ongoing WhatsApp glitch means photos you send are less private than you think
item.description: WhatsApp is one of the most trusted sites used by Gen Z. According to the company, more than half of Americans between the ages of 18 and 35 have the app on their devices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bregel, Fast Company

item.title: Apple's new AirPods Pro 2 to double as hearing aids
item.description: Apple's new AirPods Pro model is expected to double as a "clinical grade" hearing device, the company announced Monday as part of a larger product rollout.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cars collect troves of data about traffic and road hazards. Should they share it?
item.description: The secret to avoiding red lights during rush hour in Utah's largest city might be as simple as following a bus.
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former director of Los Alamos National Laboratory dead after car crash in New Mexico
item.description: A former top official in U.S. nuclear weapons research at Lawrence Livermore and Los Alamos national laboratories has died after an automobile crash in New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris walks tightrope when it comes to taxes
item.description: Democrats say Vice President Harris’s decision to offer a more modest capital gains tax hike on the wealthy is a step in the right direction to win over voters concerned she’s too liberal or too antagonistic to business.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, The Hill

item.title: Where the next billion creatives will come from
item.description: Former Hewlett Packard CEO Carly Fiorina described China as an imitator nation in her interview with Time in 2015: I have been doing business in China for decades, and I will tell you that yeah, the Chinese can take a test, but what they can’t do is innovate. They are not terribly imaginative.
item.dc:creator: Payal Arora, Fast Company

item.title: How many jobs are available in technology in the US?
item.description: The unemployment rate for tech occupations inched up to 3.4% in August from 3.2% in July, according to analysis of today’s US Bureau of Labor Statistics jobs report by tech industry group CompTIA.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden joins countries seeking to end screen time for children under 2
item.description: Sweden says children under the age of 2 should not be exposed to any digital screens. The recommendations, issued by the Scandinavian country’s public health agency earlier this month as a new school year begins, are the latest in a worldwide effort to limit screen time for young children.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Telegram CEO defends himself against French charges in first public comments
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Telegram founder and CEO Pavel Durov promised to step up efforts to fight criminality on the messaging app, his first public comments since French authorities handed him preliminary charges for allegedly allowing the platform's use for criminal activity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video game performers reach agreement with 80 video games on AI terms
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — After striking for over a month, video game performers have reached agreements with 80 games that have signed interim or tiered budget agreements with the performers' union and accepted the artificial intelligence provisions they have been seeking.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Generative AI and coding: Time to rethink software development
item.description: It’s well documented that software development efforts that incorporate generative AI include mistakes that are radically different than what any human programmer would ever make.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft-Inflection deal is a merger, but that’s OK, says UK
item.description: The UK’s antitrust regulator has concluded its investigation into Microsoft’s hiring of the majority of staff from Inflection and its licensing of the company’s technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco, HPE, Dell announce support for Nvidia’s pretrained AI workflows
item.description: Cisco, HPE, Dell and others are looking to use Nvidia’s new AI microservice application-development blueprints to help enterprises streamline the deployment of generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Chief Data Officers Profiles in Excellence in Government 2024: Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence Trends
item.description: What strategies and technology are driving data strategy in government? The post Federal Executive Forum Chief Data Officers Profiles in Excellence in Government 2024: Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence Trends first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Data resilience and zero trust: Continuity of operations no matter what
item.description: How is DISA navigating cybersecurity and zero trust? The post Data resilience and zero trust: Continuity of operations no matter what first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: PostgreSQL tutorial: Get started with PostgreSQL 16
item.description: For years now, PostgreSQL has been one of the best-kept secrets of enterprise software and open source alike—a powerful, robust, expertly maintained relational database. MySQL may be better known, but PostgreSQL offers many features MySQL has only started to provide.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Indonesia arrests a fugitive former Filipino town mayor wanted for illegal online gaming scams
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A dismissed town mayor who fled the Philippines after being accused of helping establish an illegal online gaming and scam center catering mostly to clients in China has been arrested near Indonesia’s capital, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk's Starlink backtracks and says it will comply with judge's order to block X in Brazil
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Elon Musk’s satellite-based internet service provider Starlink backtracked Tuesday and said it will comply with a Brazilian Supreme Court justice's order to block the billionaire’s social media platform, X.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
